title: Vill Goir
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]|the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] [[Vill]]}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Goir.jpg]] |name=Vill Goir |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]In [[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]], Vill Goir [[Death/Legends|dies]] during a [[Skirmish on Ossus|skirmish]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of that [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{CSWECite|I|350|Goir, Vill}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Yellow]] |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Dark Empire]] **[[Dark Side Elite]] |masters=[[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] |apprentices=[[Kam Solusar]] {{C|[[Lightsaber combat/Legends|Lightsaber combat]] apprentice}}[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]] }} {{Quote|Kam Solusar, you betrayed the dark side. Your life is forfeit.|audio=YourLifeIsForfeit.flac|Vill Goir|[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]]}} '''Vill Goir''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] in the service of the [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A member of the [[Dark Side Elite]], Goir was the organization's second-in-command, subordinate only to its leader, [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Military Executor]] [[Sedriss QL]]. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Goir assisted Sedriss in the execution of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] campaign called [[Operation Shadow Hand]], participating in the [[Battle of Balmorra|failed subjugation effort]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Balmorra/Legends|Balmorra]]. Upon Palpatine's [[Transfer essence|return]] in a new clone body, Goir was dispatched with Sedriss to the planet [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] to confront [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and a defected member of the Dark Side Elite named [[Kam Solusar]]. Arriving on Ossus, Goir and Sedriss [[Skirmish on Ossus|engaged]] the two [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], with Goir attempting to take on the turncoat Solusar. However, as Goir had previously taught Solusar all his [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] techniques while they served together, the [[darksider]] was outmatched by the Jedi and [[Death/Legends|slain]] by him, [[Sai tok/Legends|sliced in half]] with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. ==Biography== ===Servant of the Emperor=== {{Quote|The Emperor has a new ''elite corps'' of dark-side warriors…they're ''very'' powerful.|Kam Solusar, revealing the existence of the Dark Side Elite to the New Republic|}} [[File:Elite1.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Vill Goir was the second-in-command of the Dark Side Elite.]] Vill Goir was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] in service to the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]], [[Transfer essence|reborn]] in a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body[[Dark Empire 2|''Dark Empire'' 2]] after his [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of the film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars:'' Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], which features [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. ruled his clandestine [[Dark Empire]] from the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. During this [[Time/Legends|time]], Palpatine solidified his power base by creating a cabal of powerful [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]—the [[Dark Side Elite]]. Consisting of exactly seven members at any given time, each personally empowered with the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] by Palpatine,[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]] the cadre was led by [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Executor]] [[Sedriss QL]]. Goir was chosen to serve as Sedriss's substitute,{{FactFile|85|SED 1-4|Exekutor Sedress (German Edition)}} assistant and overall second-in-command of the group. The Dark Side Elite was headquartered on the planet [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]], inside [[Bast Castle]], the former [[Fortress/Legends|citadel]] of Palpatine's late [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].[[Dark Empire II 6|''Dark Empire II'' 6]] While stationed there, Goir instructed one of the fellow Dark [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Kam Solusar]], in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Dark Empire 6|''Dark Empire'' 6]], which features [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] at the [[Battle of Pinnacle Base]], to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]]. the Emperor met his demise once again in [[Battle of Pinnacle Base|battle]] with [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his sister, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], near the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Pinnacle Base]].[[Dark Empire 6|''Dark Empire'' 6]] Later that same [[Standard year/Legends|year]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the defection of [[Kam Solusar]] from the [[Dark Side Elite]] to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], after the events of [[Dark Empire 6|''Dark Empire'' 6]]. Solusar defected from the Dark Side Elite, driven to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] by Skywalker. While the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] waited for Palpatine to return in another clone body, Sedriss proceeded with [[Operation Shadow Hand]], a series of campaigns aimed at conquering neutral worlds that showed defiance to the Empire. ===Battle of Balmorra=== {{Quote|Obviously, Beltane has broken the ''treaty'' with the Empire, sir! His planet deserves to ''die!''|Vill Goir, during the Battle of Balmorra|}} During one such campaign, Goir accompanied Sedriss to the [[Factory world/Legends|factory world]] of [[Balmorra/Legends|Balmorra]]. After the latest death of Palpatine, Balmorra's [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Hinch Beltane]] had started dealing with the New Republic, forcing Sedriss to intervene. Arriving aboard the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']] and joining an [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial fleet]] stationed in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of Balmorra, Goir and fellow Dark Side Elite member [[Krdys Mordi]] served as Sedriss's [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeants]]-at-arms during the campaign. [[File:ImperialHaggling-DEII1.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Vill Goir (right), Krdys Mordi (left), and Sedriss (center) contact Governor Beltane from the ''Avenger''.]] In this capacity, Goir witnessed as Sedriss contacted Beltane via [[Viewscreen/Legends|viewscreen]], demanding he place his factories under Imperial control and face execution for his [[Treason/Legends|treason]], lest the Executor would completely annihilate Balmorra. Beltane refused to accept the terms, forcing Sedriss to launch a [[Battle of Balmorra|ground assault]] on the planet. Sedriss initially believed that he would have the upper hand, as he had the newest Imperial invention at his disposal, the [[Cyborg/Legends|cybernetic]] [[Shadow Droid]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. However, Beltane countered Sedriss's arsenal with his own newest creation, the mammoth [[X-1 Viper]] [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Goir realized that Beltane had produced the droid model in secret, thus betraying Balmorra's agreements with the Empire. Outraged by such defiance, Goir called for Sedriss to order a [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|planetary bombardment]] of Balmorra in retaliation. Mordi concurred with Goir, yet Sedriss did not heed his cohorts' advice. Marveled by the Vipers' capabilities, the Executor wanted to obtain the machines to supply the Imperial wartime needs. Negotiating a peace treaty with Beltane that granted the planet freedom in exchange for a contract to buy the droids, Goir, Sedriss, Mordi, and the rest of the Imperial forces left Balmorra. ===Showdown on Ossus=== {{Quote|Cleave their fighting arms, Goir! We'll take them ''alive!''|Sedriss QL, facing Luke Skywalker and Kam Solusar|}} The three [[darksider]]s were joined by other members of the Dark Side Elite, and they all arrived on Byss in [[I-7 Howlrunner]] starfighters There, Palpatine had already taken control of his final clone body, and he revealed himself to Sedriss and took control of the Empire once again. Shortly thereafter, an Imperial [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] detected Skywalker and the defector Solusar on the planet [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]—which had once served as the headquarters of the Jedi[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 1]]—where they were researching the history of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. [[File:Elite4.jpg|thumb|right|230px|Vill Goir and Sedriss charging at the Jedi]] Goir and Sedriss took a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[darktrooper]]s, specialized [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] [[Force power/Legends|empowered]] with the dark side, and flew aboard the [[Imperial hunter (starship)|Imperial hunter]] ''[[Scourge One]]'' toward their [[Skirmish on Ossus|confrontation]] with the Jedi. As Goir's team was landing on Ossus, ''Scourge One''{{'s}} [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] detected that Skywalker and Solusar were accompanied by a large group of [[humanoid]]s armed with primitive weapons—the [[Ysanna/Legends|Ysanna]] tribe native to Ossus. Sedriss ordered Goir to unleash their dark [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] squad upon the primitives, but after a prolonged fight, the troopers were slain by the Jedi and the Ysanna. Noticing that Skywalker was weakened by the fight, Goir and Sedriss moved in to attack, utilizing [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force choke]] against their adversaries, only for the [[The Force/Legends|Force]] attack to be blocked by Skywalker. Willing to capture the Jedi alive, Sedriss ordered Goir not to kill them but only [[Cho sun/Legends|slice off their fighting arms]]. Goir and Sedriss then ignited their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] and charged at the Jedi. Goir attacked Solusar, intent on bringing the defector to justice. However, having learned all of Goir's lightsaber techniques back on Vjun, Solusar outmaneuvered the darksider and killed him by [[Sai tok/Legends|cleaving him in half]] across the torso. Sedriss himself was killed by the ancient [[Neti/Legends|Neti]] Jedi [[Ood Bnar]], who had spent [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] in a [[Tree/Legends|tree]]-like hibernation state on Ossus. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|''Your Excellency!'' I have just been informed that something ''terrible'' has happened! ''Sedriss'' and ''Goir'' are dead! Murdered by the Jedi Skywalker and Solusar.''"
"''''Skywalker?'' He is turning my dark-side secrets ''against'' me, I am ''certain'' of it! I taught him ''too well!'' First Katth and Fass…now Sedriss and Goir. Without my ''darkside warriors'', Skywalker may well triumph!|Emperor Palpatine's reaction to Vill Goir's demise|}} After being notified of the deaths of Goir and Sedriss, Palpatine appointed his [[Advisor/Legends|advisors]]{{CSWECite|III|227|Tedryn-Sha}} [[Xecr Nist]] and [[Tedryn-Sha]] as their replacements, with Nist becoming the new Executor and Sha taking on Goir's position of second-in-command. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I don't understand what you're planning, Executor Sedriss. We should vaporize the Balmorrans and be done with them!''"
"''Watch your tongue, Goir, before I watch it wriggling in my hand!|audio=WatchYourTongueGoir.flac|Vill Goir and Sedriss QL|}} [[File:Goir_death.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Vill Goir heedlessly taught his every lightsaber technique to Kam Solusar, resulting in his death on Ossus at the hands of his former student.]] Vill Goir was a Dark Side Adept who pledged his allegiance to Emperor Palpatine. He was considered to be the highest-ranking member of the Dark Side Elite after Sedriss QL, and he was unafraid to argue with the Executor and voice his opinion, believing in the necessity of the orbital bombardment of Balmorra. However, he respected his superior and demanded that the Jedi on Ossus address Sedriss by his full title. Goir intended to bring the defector Kam Solusar to justice, failing to realize that the latter knew all of his tricks and charging at him recklessly to his death. Goir was a light-skinned individual with a [[Color/Legends|yellow]] glow in his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. ==Powers and abilities== [[File:Sedriss_Goir_Force_choke.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Vill Goir and Sedriss use their mastery of Force choke against Luke Skywalker and Kam Solusar.]] {{Quote|My throat! I can feel the cartilage snapping…''"
"''That's an old Sith trick. It's deadly, but I know how to block it.|audio=MyThroat.flac|Kam Solusar and Luke Skywalker, while being Force-choked by Vill Goir and Sedriss QL|}} Immersed in the dark side of the Force, Vill Goir was a Dark Jedi and an extension of his Emperor's will. Goir knew how to perform certain dark side techniques, employing the Force choke ability against Luke Skywalker and Kam Solusar. He was adept at lightsaber combat, having instructed Solusar on Vjun. That fact later played against him, when Solusar used Goir's own tricks to best him. ==Equipment== Dressed in traditional [[Clothing/Legends|garments]] of the Dark Side Elite, Goir wore black [[Robe/Legends|robes]] complete with a long [[Cape/Legends|cape]], as well as heavy [[Boot/Legends|boots]] and [[Gauntlet/Legends|gauntlets]]. In combat, Goir wielded a lightsaber. Like the rest of the Dark Side Elite, he [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] an I-7 Howlrunner. ==Behind the scenes== Vill Goir debuted in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] [[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]], the first issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire II]]'' series, written by [[Tom Veitch]], illustrated [[Cam Kennedy]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[December 20]], [[1994]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54063/star_wars_dark_empire_ii_1994_1|text=Star Wars: Dark Empire II (1994) #1}} He also appeared in the [[Dark Empire II 3|third issue]], which featured his death. Goir was originally set to be introduced in Veitch's proposed short novel [[Lightsider (story)|''Lightsider'']], which would have taken place before the events of ''Dark Empire II''. However, ''Lightsider'' was canceled and was never officially published.{{CollectorCite|4|ComicScan|ComicScan: Comics That Almost Were}} Goir also featured in the [[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]], in which he was voiced by [[John Cygan]].''[[Dark Empire: The Collector's Edition]]'' The audio drama differs slightly in the events pertaining to Goir. During the Battle of Balmorra, Goir is the only adviser accompanying Sedriss, with Mordi being absent from the scene. Goir is also significantly more outspoken regarding Sedriss's decision not to utilize planetary bombardment. The battle itself is also provoked by Beltane, who shoots at Sedriss's fleet with planetary [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]]. ==Appearances== *[[Dark Empire II (audio drama)|''Dark Empire II'' audio drama]] *[[Dark Empire II 1|''Dark Empire II'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]] *[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]] {{Mo}} ===Non-canon appearances=== *[[Lightsider (story)|''Lightsider'']] {{C|Unpublished}} {{C|canceled}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Vill Goir}} *{{GalaxyCite|8|Man of War ...and Peace}} {{Po}} *{{CollectorCite|4|ComicScan|ComicScan: Comics That Almost Were}} *[[Star Wars Handbook 3: Dark Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 3: ''Dark Empire'']] *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{FactFile|85|SED 1-4|Exekutor Sedress (German Edition)}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{ScrollBox|content={{Reflist}}}} {{Interlang |es=Vill Goir }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Goir, Vill}} [[Category:Dark Side Elite]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Pilots]]
